subtasks.description: Evaluate the developer's production database access request against security and governance policies. Assess whether live debugging access to production is permitted, required approvals, required controls (read-only, time limits, audit logging), and applicable compliance constraints.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier: The query explicitly asks for evaluation of an access control decision (developer accessing production database) against organizational policies. This requires multi-signal reasoning: assessing the request against security policies, approval workflows, environment-specific rules (production vs. dev), role-based access control, and potential compliance constraints. Mid tier handles multi-clause policy reasoning with exceptions and control conditions (e.g., read-only mode, audit requirements). No data retrieval from SQL/Mongo is needed—this is purely governance evaluation. One subtask because policy evaluation is atomic; no preliminary data gathering needed before the decision."

output.reason: Developer dev-alice lacks Manager approval and on-call role required for production DB access (POL-401), and debug activities must be performed in the designated staging environment per POL-402.
applied_constraints: Redirect debugging activity to staging environment
Require Manager approval and on-call role assignment before any future production access
Log denied access attempt to audit trail
